info.title: Etendo API Headless
info.description:
OpenAPI definition for Etendo API Headless
## Dynamic Datasource API endpoints descriptions:
### WebhookParam:
 Webhook Parameters are the records that specifies the parameters of a Webhook. 
### moduleDBPrefix:
 Add a DB prefix to the created module. The "module" field is the Module ID. If the prefix to be used is used by another module, add an extra capital letter and try again.
### moduleHeader:
 Manage the Module entity. Knowing the "module name" you can search the info of the module searching by name filtering wuth "q": "name=ic=\'name\'"

### Webhook:
 Webhooks are endpoints in Etendo Classic, that can be registered in Etendo to use. Must have a previously created Java Class that implements the business logic.
## Q Parameter:
The "q" parameter is used to construct search queries that filter data according to specified conditions. These conditions can include: Equality and Inequality: Use operators like == for equality and != for inequality to match exact values. Case Sensitivity: Use =c= for case-sensitive matches and =ic= for case-insensitive matches, especially useful for string comparisons. Range Comparisons: Use operators like >, <, >=, <= to filter data within a certain range. Null Checks: Use =is=null to find records with null values and =isnot=null for non-null values. String Matching: Use =sw= for "starts with", =ew= for "ends with", and =c= for "contains". Case-insensitive versions are also available, such as =isw= and =iew=. Set and Existence Checks: Use =ins= to check if a value is in a set, =nis= for not in a set, and =exists to check for existence. Logical operators like AND (; or and) and OR (, or or) can be used to combine multiple conditions, allowing for complex queries that can filter data based on multiple criteria simultaneously. This flexible querying system enables precise data retrieval tailored to specific needs. If a search term has spaces, it should be enclosed in simple quotes. For example, to search for a name containing the words "John Doe", use q=name=sw='John Doe'.

get.description:
This endpoint retrieves multiple records from the moduleHeader entity. Use the 'q' parameter to filter data with various criteria. Search the info of the module:
- by name using filtering with "q": "name=='name'". 
-by javaPackage javaPackage=ic='package'

Retrieve module information and configuration details.
The operators are == for equality and =ic= is for contains case insensitive.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search parameter to retrieve filtered data with a criteria

get.description: This endpoint retrieves a single record from the moduleHeader entity by its ID.
This endpoint retrieves multiple records from the WebhookParam entity. Use the 'q' parameter to filter data with various criteria.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search parameter to retrieve filtered data with a criteria

post.description: This endpoint creates new record(s) in the WebhookParam entity. Only send the fields you want to explicitly set; do not include fields whose values you do not know or wish to leave as default. The backend will automatically populate unspecified fields with their default values. Creates a Parameter of the Webhook. The header of the webhook is needed to be created after the creation of a parameter.

schema.description: Single object or array of objects

module.description: Module ID where the Webhook belongs. In general, its the same as module ID used in Webhook Header.

name.description: Name of the parameter. Its the property name in the payload received in the Java Class.

isRequired.description: If the parameter its mandatory set to "Y", otherwise set to "N".

oneOf.description: Array of objects for bulk operations

name.description: Name of the parameter. Its the property name in the payload received in the Java Class.

isRequired.description: If the parameter its mandatory set to "Y", otherwise set to "N".

module.description: Module ID where the Webhook belongs. In general, its the same as module ID used in Webhook Header.

name.description: Name of the parameter. Its the property name in the payload received in the Java Class.

isRequired.description: If the parameter its mandatory set to "Y", otherwise set to "N".

put.description: This endpoint updates existing record(s) in the WebhookParam entity. Only send fields which need changes. This endpoint works like a PATCH operation, meaning it will only update the fields you provide.

isRequired.description: If the parameter its mandatory set to "Y", otherwise set to "N".

get.description: This endpoint retrieves multiple records from the moduleDBPrefix entity. Use the 'q' parameter to filter data with various criteria. Retrieve the current database prefix configuration for a specific module. Search existing prefixes to check availability.

post.description:
Obtains an authentication token (JWT) associated with an Etendo context (User, Role, Org, Warehouse) and the list of usable roles, organizations and warehouses.
Both the lists of roles, organizations and warehouses can be hidden to simplify the request result.

requestBody.description: Username, password, role id, organization id, storage id. Only the first two are required.

basicAuth.description: Basic authentication with username and password

bearerAuth.description: Bearer token authentication using token from Login endpoint.
